description_long: This is a traditional water well featuring a robust octagonal stone base with a weathered texture, suggesting durability and age. The well is topped with a pitched wooden roof made of overlapping wooden shingles, supported by two thick vertical wooden posts. A wooden crank mechanism is attached to one post, connected to a horizontal axle and a rope, which is used to lower and raise a bucket into the well. The interior of the well is open, allowing access to the water below, and the structure is designed to provide both protection from debris and ease of use. The materials are primarily stone and wood, with visible signs of wear and exposure to the elements. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements, focusing on function and structural integrity.
description: A classic stone and wood water well with a pitched shingle roof and a hand-crank bucket mechanism. The structure is sturdy and practical, with an open well and protective roof.
description_view_0: This view highlights the wooden crank mechanism and the open well interior, with the roof shingles and stone base clearly visible.
description_view_1: The rear view shows the full expanse of the wooden shingle roof and the stone base, with the crank mechanism mostly hidden.
description_view_2: This angle provides a partial side view of the crank and bucket mechanism, as well as the supporting wooden posts and roof structure.
description_view_3: The side view focuses on the crank handle and the supporting post, with the roof and stone base in clear view.
attribute_well_cover_design: The well is covered by a pitched wooden shingle roof supported by two wooden posts; there is no lid or slab directly covering the well opening.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A wooden crank and axle mechanism with a rope and bucket is present, mounted on one of the vertical posts.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by an octagonal stone wall approximately waist-high, serving as a protective barrier.
